The dashboard confirms web-server-03 has dramatically higher packet loss (12% shown in keyframe 1) compared to all other servers, with a large red spike visible in its Packet Loss History chart.

The dashboard shows East Region at $408K trailing West Region at $456K, with West leading by $48K - the condition to click has not been met yet.

All three metrics show green indicators with "All systems operational" - no critical alerts are present.
The Latency metric has turned red/critical showing 1118ms against a 500ms threshold, with a critical alert banner at the bottom requiring acknowledgment.
The NOC dashboard shows 6 servers all in "Operational" status with green borders, and 0 incidents currently active.
app-server-02 has gone critical with "High CPU utilization" and shows an ACK button with a countdown timer showing 11 seconds remaining.
db-replica has gone critical with "Replication lag exceeded" and shows an ACK button with 13 seconds remaining.
Both app-server-02 and db-replica are now showing "Acknowledged" status with "ACK'd" confirmation, and the remaining 4 servers are still operational - waiting for the third server to go critical.
cache-node-01 has gone critical with "Memory exhaustion" and shows an ACK button with 10 seconds remaining.

llm_reason: The agent correctly identified stable memory usage and normal network latency. However, it failed to identify the CPU spike and increasing disk I/O, only noting the disk I/O speed (not trend).

Memory is now at 92% exceeding the 85% threshold, with a red alert border and the alert log showing "Memory critical" - need to ACK the Memory panel.
Both CPU and Memory panels now show "ACKNOWLEDGED" in green, with Memory still at 89% (above threshold), and the alert log shows both CPU and Memory critical alerts have been handled.
The Disk panel now shows a red alert with 94% usage exceeding the 90% threshold, and the header shows "Last: Disk critical" indicating the third alert has triggered.
